title: tilia americana subsp. caroliniana
intro: Tilia americana subsp. caroliniana, also known as the American basswood or Carolina basswood, is a subspecies of the American basswood tree that is native to the southeastern United States. This deciduous tree has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Tilia americana subsp. caroliniana include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, promote relaxation, and improve sleep quality, making it a popular ingredient in herbal teas and infusions. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves, flowers, or bark in hot water or a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
